title: Asukas attitude in the film regression
display_title: Asukas attitude in the film regression
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's attitude in the film = regression             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Tue Oct 27, 2015 9:14 am Am I the only one to think that Asuka's attitude in the film, when she gets contact once again with her mother, is utterly stupid, and shows she's in complete regression ? As regression is hardly an inner progress, no wonder that she got defeated in such a way.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Oct 27, 2015 9:53 am The title to the song that plays at the moment of her epiphany is in fact translated to "Emergency Evacuation into Regression".

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Oct 27, 2015 9:59 am That is in fact the entire point of the scene.

[]: Re: Asuka's attitude in the film = regression             Quote Post by The Cruel » Tue Oct 27, 2015 10:20 am mehdi_saada wrote: Am I the only one to think that Asuka's attitude in the film, when she gets contact once again with her mother, is utterly stupid, and shows she's in complete regression ? As regression is hardly an inner progress, no wonder that she got defeated in such a way.

[]:             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Tue Oct 27, 2015 11:58 am Oh my ! Glad lot of people think the same, and that is indeed intended by the author. I didn't know for the song. Superb fusion between symbolic and storyline's elements. As I said somewhere else : she doesn't even say one time " I won't let people die ". Despicably childish, and I saw infants able to love other people that their parents, and care for them, which doesn't seem to be her case, or at least this feeling is far from dominating her thoughts. Still, why would the SEELE care for her state of mind ? Do you think they are sadistic, besides their death seeking will ? I wonder too, how people could continue appreciating Asuka after such a ridiculous epiphany ? Eventhought the whole character is a fail ? She didn't even go bersek, that way she could have destroyed more than one Koa ...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Oct 27, 2015 12:46 pm mehdi_saada wrote: As I said somewhere else : she doesn't even say one time " I won't let people die ". Despicably childish, and I saw infants able to love other people that their parents, and care for them, which doesn't seem to be her case, or at least this feeling is far from dominating her thoughts.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Tue Oct 27, 2015 1:22 pm mehdi_saada wrote: Oh my ! Glad lot of people think the same, and that is indeed intended by the author. I didn't know for the song. Superb fusion between symbolic and storyline's elements. As I said somewhere else : she doesn't even say one time " I won't let people die ". Despicably childish, and I saw infants able to love other people that their parents, and care for them, which doesn't seem to be her case, or at least this feeling is far from dominating her thoughts. Still, why would the SEELE care for her state of mind ? Do you think they are sadistic, besides their death seeking will ? I wonder too, how people could continue appreciating Asuka after such a ridiculous epiphany ? Eventhought the whole character is a fail ? She didn't even go bersek, that way she could have destroyed more than one Koa ...

[]:             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Tue Oct 27, 2015 4:59 pm Well, right, I went a little too far. Plus, you took my remark about berserk wrong: it didn't mean that she's a loser because she couldn't go berserk, as you can hardly prompt it on your own accord. I meant that her inability to go berserk during the entire series, means that her inner motivations to pilot are by itself way more egoistical that Shinji's at any time. My critics concern more what psychologic character Asuka stands for, not that much the character itself. She is just a character anyway, and not a vilain indeed.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Oct 27, 2015 5:35 pm mehdi_saada wrote: Plus, you took my remark about berserk wrong: it didn't mean that she's a loser because she couldn't go berserk, as you can hardly prompt it on your own accord. I meant that her inability to go berserk during the entire series, means that her inner motivations to pilot are by itself way more egoistical that Shinji's at any time.

[]:             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Tue Oct 27, 2015 6:00 pm Wow. You really live up to your nickname ! All what I said was to make sense on a deeper lever than the banal storyline level. We can summon lots of in-universe reasons, pretexts, explaining it, even some that would not feel too much far-fetched. Whatever. Storyline is a pretext for throwing truths at the public's face. Let us try to assume this just a moment , then the next question would be : what truth in this case ?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Oct 27, 2015 6:29 pm Bagheera wrote: Kyoko never does it, either because she can't (because she's a partial soul, or because of better control tech in Unit 02, or both) or won't.

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Oct 27, 2015 6:36 pm pwhodges wrote: He got it for a reason...

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Oct 27, 2015 6:43 pm In a word, seniority or charisma. Read here, for instance, about half way down the page.

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Oct 27, 2015 6:49 pm Thanks (Charisma eh? )

[]:             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Tue Oct 27, 2015 6:52 pm Yeah, Nigoki did it once, but that's all. She hardly got the same power Yui had in all her berserk moments, that's the thing I would like to hightlight. Even the way they want their mom back deep inside, feels strangely but vastly different to my eyes. I can't get why right now. The both of them seem to be exhibit an ordinary oedipian complex though !

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Oct 27, 2015 7:10 pm pwhodges wrote: Actually, after Unit-02 has been eviscerated by the MPEs, she reactivates to stretch out her arms towards her attackers and struggles to escape the lance, and one of the bridge bunnies shouts "berserker!" - while Asuka is repeating "I'll kill you" over and over again.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Oct 27, 2015 7:38 pm I see it a little differently. Loss of control - pretty much - and this can happen when there's power. But for me the point is the Eva using her own capability to move (there's a biological being in there, remember) that doesn't necessarily need external power . So the berserk state is when the Eva decides to step outside either or both of the constraints of control and power. In this case, she may still be reacting to Asuka's demands, but it is only by going outside the imposed control system that she is able to respond using her innate power (what remains of it after the beating she's taken). This is agency, even if it is in a sense cooperative on this occasion.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Oct 27, 2015 7:49 pm pwhodges wrote: I see it a little differently. Loss of control - pretty much - and this can happen when there's power. But for me the point is the Eva using her own capability to move (there's a biological being in there, remember) that doesn't necessarily need external power . So the berserk state is when the Eva decides to step outside either or both of the constraints of control and power. In this case, she may still be reacting to Asuka's demands, but it is only by going outside the imposed control system that she is able to respond using her innate power (what remains of it after the beating she's taken). This is agency, even if it is in a sense cooperative on this occasion.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Wed Oct 28, 2015 12:48 am Or, in this case, the Beserker is not the Eva, but Asuka herself: as you pointed out, she is in TOTAL sync with her Eva. The Eva, at this point, was moving in a manner that mimicked her actions. Thus, while the soul itself wasn't necessarily the one going beserk, there's not doubt that Asuka (blinded, in agony, and furiously fighting to stay alive) was in a state of complete and intense 'fight.' In the heat of the moment, with the readings being what they were and everything else happening, what the bunnies may have seen that looked like a beserker state wasn't, but was too close to differentiate.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 28, 2015 1:53 am Gob Hobblin wrote: Or, in this case, the Beserker is not the Eva, but Asuka herself: as you pointed out, she is in TOTAL sync with her Eva. The Eva, at this point, was moving in a manner that mimicked her actions. Thus, while the soul itself wasn't necessarily the one going beserk, there's not doubt that Asuka (blinded, in agony, and furiously fighting to stay alive) was in a state of complete and intense 'fight.' In the heat of the moment, with the readings being what they were and everything else happening, what the bunnies may have seen that looked like a beserker state wasn't, but was too close to differentiate.
